summary.what_happened: Search latency saturated from 16:05 under queue pressure; the operator scaled replicas 3->6 at 16:32 and metrics recovered by 16:45.
summary.customer_impact: Search was slow or timed out for ~40 minutes; recovered.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-25T16:20:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; end = start of sustained recovery across all deviated series

severity.explanation: p95 peaked at 940ms (5.2x baseline, inside the >4x SEV-2 band) and error rate 1.1% (inside 1-25%), but recovery was observed in-window after the operator scale-up and impact was brief - judged one level below the numeric ceiling

evidence.interpretation: change record change_om_1 16:32: operator scaled replicas 3->6 during response - a mitigation record, not a candidate cause (symptoms began 16:05, before it)
queue saturation warnings precede timeouts; the observed scaling event at 16:33 is followed by 'queue drained' at 16:44
search p95 first crossed 2x baseline at 16:05 (520ms) and recovered to within 10% of baseline from 16:45
recovery begins the interval after the 16:32 scale-up: 880ms at 16:30 -> 430ms at 16:35 -> baseline by 16:45

hypotheses.title: capacity saturation at 3 replicas, resolved by operator scale-up
hypotheses.statement: Query load saturated the 8-worker pool at 3 replicas from 16:05; the operator's 16:32 scale-up to 6 replicas was followed by drain and recovery by 16:45 - consistent with a pure capacity shortfall.
assumptions: query volume did not drop independently at 16:35
recommended_checks: compare query volume 16:00-17:00 against the previous day
confirm replica count history matches change_om_1

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-3, recovered: search latency saturated for ~40 minutes; the operator scaled replicas 3->6 at 16:32 (change_om_1) and metrics recovered by 16:45. The tool has executed nothing; the only action taken was the operator's, recorded as an observed change. Remaining options await human approval.

status_page.text: Search was slow for some users earlier today. Service has returned to normal and we are monitoring to confirm full recovery.

postmortem_draft.title: Postmortem draft: search saturation 2026-06-25
postmortem_draft.summary: Search capacity at 3 replicas was insufficient for the afternoon query load; latency saturated until the operator scaled to 6 replicas, after which queues drained and metrics recovered.
postmortem_draft.impact: Search slow/timing out ~40 minutes; fully recovered.
contributing_factors: likely: replica count sized below current query volume
open_questions: was the load organic growth or a one-off spike
action_items: keep replicas at 6 pending the volume review
